data.content:
**Yes, C# fully supports `stackalloc`'d `Span<nint>` and `Span<nuint>`.**

Both native-sized integer types work:

```csharp
Span<nint> span = stackalloc nint[4];
Span<nuint> uspan = stackalloc nuint[3];
```

The test compiled and ran successfully, confirming you can stack-allocate spans of `nint` (signed) and `nuint` (unsigned) native-sized integers. This has been supported since C# 9 / .NET 5, when `nint`/`nuint` were introduced as first-class types.
